q:
Which driver has priority at this narrow passage?

A. The one who sees the red sign.
B. The one who sees the blue sign.
e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
Red sign: giving way.
Blue sign: priority.
clean_q:
Which driver has priority at this narrow passage? 
A. The one who sees the red sign. 
B. The one who sees the blue sign.
clean_e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
Red sign: giving way.
Blue sign: priority.

q: There is no oncoming traffic. Can you continue driving?
e: You have to wait until the traffic light is green.
clean_q: There is no oncoming traffic. Can you continue driving?
clean_e: You have to wait until the traffic light is green.

q:
Two cars will cross each other on a dirt road.
How much lateral distance must be kept?

A. 1 meter.
B. 1.5 meters.
C. Sufficient lateral distance.
e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
The traffic regulations don’t define 'how much' it must be. There is written: 'sufficient'.
clean_q:
Two cars will cross each other on a dirt road.
How much lateral distance must be kept?
A. 1 meter.
B. 1.5 meters.
C. Sufficient lateral distance.
clean_e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
The traffic regulations don’t define 'how much' it must be. There is written: 'sufficient'.

q:
The road is very narrow. Is the blue car allowed to drive a bit on the cycle lane to ease the passing?

A. No.
B. Yes.
C. Yes, only when he doesn’t hinder other road users.
e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
The bicycle lane is not part of the roadway. You may not drive on it.
clean_q:
The road is very narrow. Is the blue car allowed to drive a bit on the cycle lane to ease the passing? 
A. No.
B. Yes.
C. Yes, only when he doesn’t hinder other road users.
clean_e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
The bicycle lane is not part of the roadway. You may not drive on it.

q:
A driver who sees this sign at a narrow passage, must give way to approaching traffic.

A. Correct.
B. Incorrect.
e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
Along the opposite direction is a blue sign.

A driver who sees this sign at a narrow passage, must give way to approaching traffic.

q:
Who is allowed to drive first under the bridge?

A. You.
B. The oncoming traffic.
e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
You see a sign that says that you should give priority.
clean_q:
Who is allowed to drive first under the bridge? 
A. You.
B. The oncoming traffic.
clean_e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
You see a sign that says that you should give priority.

q:
Roadworks. Which car has priority?

A. The blue car.
B. The red car.
e:
LESSON 14
If there are no traffic signs regulating priority, the driver on the side where the obstacle is located must give way.
In this example, the blue car may pass the road narrowing first, because the obstacle is on the side of the red car.
clean_q:
Roadworks. Which car has priority?
A. The blue car.
B. The red car.
clean_e:
LESSON 14
If there are no traffic signs regulating priority, the driver on the side where the obstacle is located must give way.
In this example, the blue car may pass the road narrowing first, because the obstacle is on the side of the red car.

q:
Who must give priority?

A. The brown car.
B. The blue car.
e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
There is no priority sign.
The driver along whose side the road narrows, has to give way.
clean_q:
Who must give priority? 
A. The brown car.
B. The blue car.
clean_e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
There is no priority sign.
The driver along whose side the road narrows, has to give way.

LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
It may, on condition that you don’t disturb the other drivers.

LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.

LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
You are not allowed to drive there in order to pass a vehicle.

q:
What do these cars have to do at this junction?

A. They pass each other on the right.
B. They pass each other on the left.
e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
Arrows on the ground indicate that they should pass each other on the left.
If there are no arrows, then they pass each other on the right.
clean_q:
What do these cars have to do at this junction? 
A. They pass each other on the right. 
B. They pass each other on the left.
clean_e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
Arrows on the ground indicate that they should pass each other on the left.
If there are no arrows, then they pass each other on the right.

q:
Who has priority?

A. Car A.
B. Car B.
e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
The road narrows along the side of car A. Therefore car A has to give priority.

Who has priority?
A. Car A.

q:
A bike suggestion lane.
When passing a car …

A. ... you are not allowed to drive on it.
B. ... you are allowed to drive on it.
C. ... you must drive on it.
e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
A bike suggestion lane (reserved cycle lane) is not a bicycle lane, but part of the roadway.
You have to drive on the right side of the roadway, so on this strip.

clean_e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
A bike suggestion lane (reserved cycle lane) is not a bicycle lane, but part of the roadway.
You have to drive on the right side of the roadway, so on this strip.

LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
The blue sign says you have priority.

q:
Is the driver of the blue car allowed to drive on the reserved cycle lane in order to ease the passing?

A. Yes, he is.
B. He isn’t only allowed to, he must do it.
C. No, he isn’t.
e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
A bicycle suggestion strip or reserved cycle lane is not a bicycle lane, but is part of the carriageway and the driver must drive on the right side of the road.
clean_q:
Is the driver of the blue car allowed to drive on the reserved cycle lane in order to ease the passing? 
A. Yes, he is.
B. He isn’t only allowed to, he must do it. 
C. No, he isn’t.
clean_e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
A bicycle suggestion strip or reserved cycle lane is not a bicycle lane, but is part of the carriageway and the driver must drive on the right side of the road.

What does this traffic sign mean?

A. One way traffic.
B. Ahead only.
e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
Meaning: one-way traffic.
Information sign.

What does this traffic sign mean? 
A. One way traffic.

clean_e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.

Information sign.

q: Is a driver allowed to drive on the levelled verge to ease the passing?
e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
He may drive over the berm along the way.
He may not drive on a cycle lane or on the part of the public road where cars can wait and park.

LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.

q:
This sign indicates a narrow passage.
What kind of sign is it?

A. An information sign.
B. A danger sign.
C. A priority sign.
e:
LESSON 14 – ‘Crossing’.
Triangular signs with a red border are usually warning or danger signs.
Warning signs stand 150 meters for a danger.
